title: L1 PM5 Shearing Pin Issue
reproduction:
1. Configure PM5 as Single Plan
2. Launch TM and PM3
3. Exit PM5 and the configuration
4. Run wafer through PM3
analysis: When singlePlanMotio option change it requires TM and PM must both reboot. Log shows only PM was rebooted, not TM. This causes the shearing pin fault when robot tries to pick wafer from misaligned position.

title: L1 PM3 AEI Baseline File Missing
reproduction:
1. Load new recipe without setting HydraBaselineAEIFilename
2. Run wafer through PM3
3. Observe PreEtch Step 31 failure
analysis: HydraBaselineAEIFilename left empty in recipe. AEI Predicted Difference Map tries to compute (current - baseline) but baseline is nil. nil.asNumber raises 'Message not understood: #asNumber'. Fix: register baseline AEI file in recipe config or disable HydraAutoCalEnable.

title: JIT Stuck due to WAC Disabled Configuration
reproduction:
1. Set flow with WAC
2. Select WAC as disabled in configuration
3. Start process and observe next wafer advancement
analysis: Flow with WAC selected as disabled caused JIT misjudgment, preventing the next wafer from advancing[cite: 2]. Solution is to check for receiveDisablingTags #WAC when querying for WAC time

title: JIT Stoppage on Step 0 Interruption and Abort Recovery
reproduction:
1. Run flow with no WAC
2. Create redAlarm in PM before start of Process (Step 0)
3. Select 'Abort and Remove' in alarm recovery
analysis: New JIT rule did not consider Process fail/interrupted abort and remove recovery option at step 0[cite: 3]. Fixed by updating logic to check if there is WAC after an interrupt at Process

title: JIT Rule Missing WAC Abort Recovery at Step 0
reproduction:
1. Run flow with PreWAC/WAC/PostWAC
2. Create redAlarm in PM before start of WAC cycles (Step 0)
3. Select 'Abort PreWAC/WAC/PostWAC' in alarm recovery
analysis: JIT rule failed to handle abort recovery specifically for PreWAC/WAC/PostWAC cycles interrupted at step 0[cite: 4, 5]. Logic updated to check for Wac = 0 and handle associated alarm events

title: VC Job Wafer Premature Pick and Place causing Abort
reproduction:
1. Manually start AEW (PreWac = 60s)
2. Run production job with VC material (VC93/VC94)
3. Observe if Pick/Place starts before PreConditionWAC tag
analysis: Job wafer started Pick and Place too early (before PreWac start), causing scheduler to abort operations when PreWac tag was added to prevent slot door timeouts[cite: 8, 9, 10]. Solution ensures #PreConditionWAC happens before Pick/Place starts[cite: 21].
